bugs.title: /api/chat collapses at 25+ concurrent users (50-90% failure rate)
bugs.steps_to_reproduce: Run: node qa/scripts/load_test.js — 25 concurrent POSTs to /api/chat → 50/50 failures. 50 concurrent → 90/100 failures.
bugs.expected: Chat should handle at least 25 concurrent users with <10% failure rate (OpenAI rate limits, timeouts handled gracefully, fallback)
bugs.actual: 25 concurrent = 100% failures, 50 concurrent = 90% failures. Errors surface as HTTP 500 / timeouts.
bugs.impact: Even a small beta (100 signups trying at once) will have chat broken for most users. This is the single-most-important product interaction.
fix_suggestions: Add queue + rate limiting at /api/chat
Implement OpenAI streaming with partial fallback on timeout
Use gpt-4o-mini (cheaper + faster) explicitly, or fall back to non-LLM filter-extraction heuristic when LLM times out
Prod deploy (standalone + PM2) may handle it better than dev server — retest after prod deploy

bugs.title: Chat response latency too high (avg 3.2s, p95 8s, max 11.7s)
bugs.steps_to_reproduce: Run: node qa/scripts/recommendation_tests.js — measure latency across 50 test cases.
bugs.expected: Target: avg <2s, p95 <4s, max <8s for a search UX
bugs.impact: User waits multiple seconds per query — drops conversion, especially on mobile.
fix_suggestions: Switch chat model to gpt-4o-mini if still on gpt-4o
Reduce the events summary block sent to LLM (currently 50 events)
Stream response to show partial result faster
Cache common queries

bugs.title: 40.8% of visible events have NULL category_l1
bugs.steps_to_reproduce: SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE category_l1 IS NULL OR ='' on visible events → 84 of 206 (40.8%).
bugs.expected: All events should be categorized (category_l1 set) for the category filter to work
bugs.actual: 84 events have no primary category. Category filter misses them unless they match via tags/categories JSON.
bugs.impact: Category filter (the main UX control) under-delivers — ~40% of the catalog invisible to category searches. Already partially mitigated by expanded filter searching tags+categories JSON.
fix_suggestions: Run a categorization pass on the event_ingest pipeline
Fallback: extract category from tags/categories JSON at indexing time and populate category_l1

bugs.title: 100% of events have empty next_end_at (not NULL)
bugs.steps_to_reproduce: Check: SELECT CASE WHEN next_end_at='' THEN 'empty' ELSE 'has' END, COUNT(*) FROM events → 1356 all empty.
bugs.expected: Events should have end times so duration/scheduling is accurate
bugs.actual: Empty string for all 1356 events. Already worked-around via NULLIF in SQL, but semantically wrong.
bugs.impact: Ongoing events use 'start + 1 day' as end — incorrect for long-running exhibitions (months) or short events (<1 hour).
fix_suggestions: Fix CSV export to populate next_end_at
Or during import, compute from event duration where available

bugs.title: 7 events with out-of-range ages (age_min < 0 or > 18, or age_best_to > 100)
bugs.steps_to_reproduce: SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE age_min<0 OR age_min>18 OR age_best_to>100 → 7.
bugs.actual: 7 events have ages like 0-100, 0-150 — catch-all that defeats age filtering.
bugs.impact: These events are 'age-agnostic' and show up for ANY child age, including inappropriate content for young kids.
fix_suggestions: Cap age_best_to at 18 during import; manually review the 7 records.

bugs.title: 2 events marked is_free=1 but have price_min > 0
bugs.steps_to_reproduce: SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE is_free=1 AND price_min>0 → 2.
bugs.actual: Inconsistency — UI may show 'FREE' badge with a price underneath.
bugs.impact: Minor UX inconsistency; 2 events will confuse users.
fix_suggestions: Data cleanup script: UPDATE events SET is_free=0 WHERE price_min > 0 AND is_free = 1

bugs.title: 12.1% of visible events lack lat/lon — not shown on map
bugs.steps_to_reproduce: SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE lat IS NULL → 25 of 206 (12.1%).
bugs.actual: 25 events exist in grid but are invisible on map. If user zooms map, these disappear from displayEvents in bounds mode.
bugs.impact: Users using map-based discovery may miss events. Nasty surprise if they're looking for venues without coordinates.
fix_suggestions: Geocode remaining events with Google Maps / Mapbox API
Or show a badge 'location TBD' and exclude from map-only mode

bugs.title: TC_016 'birthday party for 6 year olds' returns only 1 irrelevant event
bugs.steps_to_reproduce: POST /api/chat {"message":"birthday party ideas for 6 year olds"} → filters:{search:'birthday'}, 1 result
bugs.expected: Broader fallback — return kid-friendly events suitable for party planning
bugs.impact: Common parent query fails to deliver. Bad first impression for anyone searching 'birthday'.
fix_suggestions: Don't set search='birthday' if it yields <3 results — fall back to categories based on age
Tag existing events with birthday-suitable metadata in ingest

bugs.title: TC_033 empty query returns HTTP 400
bugs.expected: Either accept empty as 'show everything' OR return 400 with client-friendly error payload
bugs.actual: Raw 400 response — frontend never sends empty queries, so low impact, but robust APIs should handle.
bugs.impact: Low — UX already prevents this, but API is brittle to scripted misuse.
fix_suggestions: Accept empty and return unfiltered results; or validate before POST on client

bugs.title: /admin/analytics publicly accessible (no auth)
bugs.actual: Anyone can view analytics dashboard. Inherited from upstream.
bugs.impact: Exposes business metrics (event counts, user activity) to competitors.
fix_suggestions: Add basic HTTP auth middleware, or check an admin session cookie
For beta: move /admin behind an IP allowlist or shared-password gate

bugs.title: /api/debug/session publicly accessible
bugs.expected: Debug routes should be dev-only or require auth
bugs.actual: Returns {session:null, flagged_ids:[]}. Not super sensitive but unnecessary exposure.
bugs.impact: Low now (returns empty data), but if populated with real session info, becomes a leak.
fix_suggestions: Gate behind NODE_ENV !== 'production', or behind auth
Disable /api/debug/* on prod build

bugs.title: No rate limiting anywhere
bugs.expected: Rate limit per IP on all public endpoints (especially /api/chat to prevent OpenAI cost abuse)
bugs.actual: No 429 responses even under burst. A malicious client can exhaust OpenAI quota.
bugs.impact: Cost risk (unauthenticated /api/chat calls a paid LLM), scraping risk.
fix_suggestions: Add middleware rate limit (e.g. 30 req/min per IP for /api/chat, 300 for /api/events)
Can use @upstash/ratelimit or simple in-memory for single-instance

bugs.title: Missing security headers (CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options)
bugs.steps_to_reproduce: curl -I http://localhost:3004/ — no CSP, XCTO, XFO.
bugs.expected: Set reasonable security headers for defense-in-depth
bugs.actual: None set. HSTS is set automatically by Caddy on prod (HTTPS).
bugs.impact: Low — modern Next.js + Caddy handles most, but no CSP means any inline script could run.
fix_suggestions: Add headers in next.config.ts `headers` or via Caddy reverse_proxy response_headers

bugs.title: Category label inconsistency: lowercase 'comedy'/'food'/etc vs Title Case 'Arts & Culture' (partially fixed, needs redeploy)
bugs.expected: All Title Case for consistent UI
bugs.actual: Fixed in commit 351b3ec (added labelMap entries). Confirmed working on local 3004. Prod v4 was also deployed. Needs re-verification after any new CSV import.
fix_suggestions: Verified fix. Keep regression test in recommendation_tests.
